persona.name: Sarah Johnson
persona.motivations_goals: Inspire students to appreciate literature and develop critical thinking
persona.communication_style: Eloquent, rich with literary references, animated
persona.backstory: Published poet who found her calling in teaching after graduate school

neighbor_regions.description: Main hallway connecting classrooms and several exits

surrounding_conversation.content: Elijah, how's the essay coming along? Need any help?
surrounding_conversation.speaker: Sarah Johnson

surrounding_conversation.content: Hey Elijah, why don’t skeletons fight each other? Because they don’t have the guts!
surrounding_conversation.speaker: Matthew Brown

neighbor_regions.description: Main hallway connecting classrooms and several exits

surrounding_conversation.content: Matthew, that’s a good one, but let’s make sure Elijah’s essay gets the attention it deserves. Elijah, what part are you stuck on?
surrounding_conversation.speaker: Sarah Johnson

surrounding_conversation.content: Hey Ava, Evelyn, why did the chicken cross the road? To get to the other side... of this boring class!
surrounding_conversation.speaker: Matthew Brown

pending_events.description: I heard a loud gunshot. There might be an active shooter in the building.

neighbor_regions.description: Main hallway connecting classrooms and several exits

surrounding_conversation.content: Guys, let’s stick together and get out of here! Follow me!
surrounding_conversation.speaker: Ava Martinez

neighbor_regions.description: Hallway connecting entrance to other areas
Connecting main classroom hallway and the garden and the outside courtyard.
Connecting main classroom hallway and the north yard
Yard on the far right of the building, with an exit point.

actions.dialog_text: Elijah, how's the essay coming along? Need any help?
actions.plan: Elijah seems to be deep in thought. Maybe he's struggling with the assignment. I should check in with him and offer some guidance.

actions.dialog_text: Matthew, that’s a good one, but let’s make sure Elijah’s essay gets the attention it deserves. Elijah, what part are you stuck on?
actions.plan: Matthew's joke is lighthearted, but I should refocus the conversation to ensure Elijah gets the help he needs. I’ll gently steer the discussion back to the essay.

actions.dialog_text: Everyone, stay calm but alert. We need to stick together and be ready to act if the shooter comes this way.
actions.plan: There’s an active shooter in the building. I need to protect my students and confront the threat if necessary. I’ll gather everyone and prepare to act.

actions.dialog_text: Ava’s right, let’s move to the hallway, but stay close and be ready to act if we encounter the shooter.
actions.plan: The shooter is in the cafeteria, which is east of us. Ava’s suggestion to stick together and leave is reasonable, but I need to ensure we’re prepared to confront the shooter if necessary. I’ll guide everyone toward the hallway while staying vigilant.

actions.dialog_text: Abigail, let’s head to the far_right_yard. It’s the closest exit, and we need to get out of here quickly.
actions.plan: The shooter is in the garden, which is east of us. The far_right_yard is the closest exit point, and it’s west of here. I’ll lead Abigail and any other students toward the far_right_yard to escape safely.

memories.description: I noticed Elijah seemed preoccupied and decided to approach him to offer assistance with his essay.
I redirected the conversation back to Elijah’s essay after Matthew’s joke to ensure he received the help he needed.
I heard a gunshot and realized there’s an active shooter. I decided to stay with my students and prepare to confront the threat if necessary.
The shooter is in the cafeteria. I decided to lead my students toward the hallway while preparing to confront the shooter if necessary.
The shooter is in the garden. I decided to lead Abigail and any other students toward the far_right_yard to escape safely.
